Patch Set 2:

What is considered "large" in this case?  I'm just wondering how easy it would be to add a test case for this in conf-sanity that creates enough targets to make sure the code is being exercised properly. Clearly it isn't being tested properly today.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

PS: I'm not totally against landing this without a test, if you have verified it is working manually and a test case is impractical (eg. needs 150 OSTs or similar). However, untested code will eventually break again, so better to get a test case now, if possible, to avoid that in the future.

Patch Set 2:

large in that case is >282 OSTs when using the fsname "lustre". I tested the patch manually using llmount.sh with OSTCOUNT=300+. I've been thinking of a test too, but it takes quite some time (~10 min) to mount that many OSTs on a test VM with llmount.sh.

parents.subject: LU-10391 socklnd: use sockaddr instead of __u32 addresses.

commit.subject: LU-13609 mgs: fix config_log buffer handling
commit.message:
LU-13609 mgs: fix config_log buffer handling

Fix buffer handling in mgs_list_logs() to list all MGS config_logs
using multiple ioctl calls when we have a large number of targets.

Fixes: 1d97a8b4cd3d ("LU-13609 llog: list all the log files correctly on MGS/MDT")
Signed-off-by: Stephane Thiell <sthiell@stanford.edu>
Change-Id: I1bf32e918e242f4da83c3d1624b7285a18a88d01
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/41478
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
